title: dracunculus divaricatus
intro: Dracunculus divaricatus, commonly known as the poisonous dragon arum or wild arum, is a plant species in the family Arum. It is a perennial with a unique appearance, featuring a hooded spathe and a spadix that bears red berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Eastern Europe and Asia, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Dracunculus divaricatus include relief from respiratory issues, skin conditions, and wounds, and it has been prepared in various herbal forms, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures.
